claims.conclusion: The exact causal claim is false for pinned redb 4.1.0: redb removed its mmap backend and uses positioned file I/O plus a userspace cache. The five-run NBVERIFY-001 benchmark proves microsecond-class measured operations and in-process use has no network hop, but neither fact rescues the asserted mmap/zero-copy mechanism or makes arbitrary thought/trace writes microsecond-safe.
primary_pointers: NBVERIFY-001 redb source/tests and five-run benchmark

claims.conclusion: A background outbox worker can technically flush selected local records to PostgreSQL, but no current CodeDB/envctl call path implements the claimed per-agent-cycle compression and permanent commit. Thread ownership, final-record identity, sequence keys, compression/version identity, transaction scope, acknowledgment, retry, idempotency, and crash recovery remain absent.

claims.conclusion: redb can back a durable outage queue or outbox, but it is not PostgreSQL WAL and no implemented protocol proves indefinite retention until connectivity returns. Capacity, backpressure, ordering, duplicates, conflict resolution, poison records, split brain, replay acknowledgment, and authority remain undefined.
